source: GloVe- Global Vectors for Word Representation

concepts.finding: Global log-bilinear regression model that trains on word-word co-occurrence counts. Combines advantages of matrix factorization (global statistical efficiency) and local context window methods (linear substructure). Achieved 75% on word analogy task. Ratio of co-occurrence probabilities (not probabilities themselves) encodes meaning.
concepts.implication: For keyword embedding from text corpora, GloVe's use of global co-occurrence statistics provides efficient training. The ratio P(k|keyword1)/P(k|keyword2) being the meaningful signal suggests keyword-intent relationships can be captured via co-occurrence ratios with context words.
concepts.open_question: Is GloVe or word2vec better for keyword-level (not word-level) embedding? How to construct keyword-keyword co-occurrence matrices?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: GloVe embeddings on keyword co-occurrence matrices will produce more stable intent clusters than word2vec on the same data due to GloVe's global statistical efficiency.
